Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - True GUI menu (no terminal emulation).
    ' - Assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - This code will enforce the layout at runtime.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (unchanged)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I" : NotYet("Backup Price List & Rolodex (implement in .NET)")
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: S, U, V, W, +, 5

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

messages.content:
In your GWBASIC program, **Typewriter Mode** is the block starting at **line 2150** (triggered by menu choice **X**: `IF Q$="X" THEN 2150`).

Functionally, it turns the program into a simple **keystroke-to-printer “typewriter”**:

- **2150**: Sends printer control codes: `LPRINT CHR$(27);"i";CHR$(1)`  
  - `CHR$(27)` is ESC. The exact effect depends on the printer (often selects a mode like “italic/on” or some printer feature). It’s printer-specific.

- **2160–2180**: Clears the screen and shows instructions and a ruler line:
  - `[TAB = Move to Right 5 Spaces] [ESC = Exit]`
  - Prints a guide line `....A....B....C...` to show column positions.

- Then it loops reading **one key at a time** using `INKEY$`:
  - **ESC** (`CHR$(27)`): exits typewriter mode, and sends `LPRINT CHR$(27);"@"` (often “reset printer”), then returns to the main menu.
  - **TAB** (`CHR$(9)`): it *doesn’t* insert a real tab—your code replaces it with a **single space** (`Q$=" "`), despite the message saying “move right 5 spaces”.
  - **ENTER** (`CHR$(13)`): sends the accumulated line `LN$` to the printer (`LPRINT LN$`), then starts a new line.
  - **Backspace** (`ASC(Q$)=8`): deletes the last character from `LN$` and redraws the line on-screen.
  - Any other character: appends to `LN$` up to 79 characters and redraws it.

So in Windows/VB.NET terms: it’s basically a **very simple text entry form** whose output goes to the **default printer**, line-by-line, when you press Enter.

If you want, I can translate it into a WinForms dialog like:
- a big `TextBox` for typing,
- Enter prints the current line (or selected text),
- Esc closes,
- and printing uses `System.Drawing.Printing.PrintDocument` instead of `LPRINT`.
remember that for later

Noted for later: **Typewriter Mode (menu X)** is the GWBASIC routine at **lines 2150–2280** that acts like a keystroke-driven “typewriter”:

- collects characters into `LN$` (max ~79 chars),
- **Enter** prints `LN$` to the printer (originally `LPRINT`),
- **Backspace** edits `LN$`,
- **Esc** exits and sends a printer reset sequence,
- shows an on-screen ruler/instructions.

When we port it to VB.NET/WinForms, we’ll implement it as a **TypewriterMode form/dialog** that prints via **`PrintDocument`** (no `LPRINT`).
what does (Y) Ed Dean's Personal Backup Do

In the GWBASIC you pasted, **(Y) Ed Dean’s Personal Backup** is just a dispatcher to another program:

- Line **1050**: `IF Q$="Y" THEN CHAIN"EDBACKUP"`

So **MainMenu.bas itself does not do the backup**. It **loads and transfers control** to a separate BASIC module/program named **`EDBACKUP`** (typically `EDBACKUP.BAS` or a compiled EXE with that basename, depending on how it was deployed).

What we can infer from *this* file alone:

- It’s intended to run a **different backup routine** than the general system backup (your “I / backup price list & rolodex” and the big ZIP-based backup around lines 1720+).
- It is likely “personal” data for “Ed Dean” (maybe separate directories/files), but the actual file list, destination, and logic are unknown without the `EDBACKUP` source.

To answer precisely (and to port it to .NET), I need the contents of the `EDBACKUP` module. If you paste `EDBACKUP.BAS`, I’ll:
1) describe exactly what it backs up and where,
2) translate it to VB.NET,
3) replace `CHAIN "EDBACKUP"` with a call into the new .NET routine/form.
no, let's keep it as is for now

Understood—**we’ll keep (Y) “Ed Dean’s Personal Backup” as a placeholder** for now and won’t implement/replace it yet.

In the current WinForms menu code, that means we leave the handler as a stub (e.g., `NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")`) until you provide `EDBACKUP` or decide how you want it handled in Windows/.NET.
what does change date or time do

In your GWBASIC, **(T) Change Date or Time** jumps to the routine starting at **line 1670** (`IF Q$="T" THEN 1670`). It does this:

1) **Clears the screen and runs an external calendar program**
- **1670**: `CLS:SHELL "CAL":LOCATE 2,1`
  - Shows a calendar (whatever DOS `CAL` is in your environment), then positions the cursor.

2) **Prompts for TIME**
- **1680**: `PRINT"TIME [Enter = ";TIME$;"] ";:INPUT Q$:IF Q$<>"" THEN TIME$=Q$`
  - Displays the current time, asks for a new time.
  - If you just press Enter, it keeps the current time.
  - If you type a value, it assigns it to `TIME$` (this sets the system time in DOS BASIC).

3) **Prompts for DATE**
- **1690**: `PRINT"DATE [Enter = ";DATE$;"] ";:INPUT Q$:IF Q$<>"" THEN DATE$=Q$`
  - Same logic for the system date.

4) **Checks for a payroll file and shows a warning**
- **1692**: `OPEN "C:\PAYROLL.CHK" FOR INPUT AS 1:CLOSE 1`
  - This is just a presence check (it will error if missing, and your ON ERROR logic handles that).
- **1693**: Prints:
  - “This change will not be permanent!”
  - Then tells you to make it permanent on the payroll main menu, waits for Enter, then returns to main menu (`GOTO 490`).

5) **There is a “make permanent” path, but it’s effectively unreachable**
- Lines **1695–1700**:
  - `1695 SHELL"ATCLOCK":REM ...`
  - `1700 GOTO 490`
- However, because **1693 always does `GOTO 490`**, execution never reaches 1695 in the code as written (unless the pasted version differs from the running one).

### What this means for VB.NET/Windows
In WinForms, we generally **should not set the system date/time** automatically (requires admin rights and is risky). A faithful port would:
- show a “Change Date/Time” dialog,
- and either:
  - open Windows Date/Time settings (recommended), or
  - attempt to set system time via Windows API if you explicitly want that and accept needing elevation.

If you want, tell me which behavior you prefer for the Windows version:
- **A)** Open Windows settings (safe)
- **B)** Actually set system date/time (requires admin/elevation)
put a place marker on that, I have to verify if that needs to be kept or not

Noted for later: **(T) Change Date or Time** is a **placeholder decision** until you verify whether it must be kept in the Windows/VB.NET port.

What it does in the GWBASIC you pasted (for your verification):
- Menu **T** jumps to **line 1670**.
- Runs external **`CAL`** (`SHELL "CAL"`).
- Prompts for **TIME** and **DATE** and, if provided, assigns to `TIME$` / `DATE$` (DOS BASIC system time/date setters).
- Checks for `C:\PAYROLL.CHK`, then warns: “This change will not be permanent! … make it permanent on the payroll main menu.” and returns to main menu.

When you’re ready, you can decide whether the VB.NET version should:
- open Windows Date/Time settings, or
- set the system clock (admin), or
- remove/disable the feature.
ok, where we're at right now with what we've done so far appears to generate the menu as buttons and seems to be working. What do we need to do next?

Next you should make the menu buttons do something real, in a way that lets us keep converting modules incrementally without breaking the app. The most practical sequence is:

## 1) Decide how “modules” will be represented in VB.NET
In GWBASIC, everything is `CHAIN "xxx"` / `SHELL` / `GOTO label`. In VB.NET WinForms, we’ll replace each menu option with one of:

- **Open a new form** (recommended): `Using f As New FormInvoiceGenerator() : f.ShowDialog() : End Using`
- **Run a translated module** in a class/module (line-by-line translation): `ModuleInvoice.Run()`
- **Temporary placeholder**: `NotYet("...")`

Pick one convention (I recommend: **one form per module**, with translated code living behind that form).

## 2) Create a “navigation + placeholder” framework (so nothing crashes)

```vbnet name=MigrationService.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Linq

Public Module MigrationService

    ' Legacy location (root of the share). Files used to be dumped here.
    Private ReadOnly LegacyRoot As String = "\\invoice"

    ' MigrationRule = Skip (per your decision)
    Public Sub EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.AppRoot)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' Move a known set of legacy file patterns from \\invoice\ to \\invoice\MainMenu\Data\
        ' Skip if destination already exists.
        Dim patterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT",
            "*.CUR",
            "*.LST",
            "*.PRC",
            "PACK.NUM",
            "BILLS.*",
            "*.CHK",
            "*.TXT"
        }

        For Each pattern In patterns
            Dim files As String() = Array.Empty(Of String)()

            Try
                files = Directory.GetFiles(LegacyRoot, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
            Catch
                ' If the share root isn't accessible or pattern fails, just continue.
                Continue For
            End Try

            For Each src In files
                Dim fileName As String = Path.GetFileName(src)
                Dim dest As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, fileName)

                ' Skip rule
                If File.Exists(dest) Then
                    Continue For
                End If

                ' Don't try to move our own folder if someone created files weirdly
                If String.Equals(src.TrimEnd("\"c), dest.TrimEnd("\"c), StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) Then
                    Continue For
                End If

                Try
                    File.Move(src, dest)
                Catch
                    ' If file is in use or permissions prevent move, skip.
                End Try
            Next
        Next
    End Sub

End Module
```

```vbnet name=BackupService.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.IO
Imports System.IO.Compression
Imports System.Linq

Public Module BackupService

    ' Implements (I) Backup Price List & Rolodex in .NET.
    ' - Sources:
    '     \\invoice\MainMenu\Data\  (data files)
    '     \\invoice\word\*.doc      (word docs)
    ' - Outputs:
    '     \\invoice\MainMenu\Backups\WORD.zip
    '     \\invoice\MainMenu\Backups\BACKUP##.zip (rotating via PACK.NUM)

    Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' 1) Backup Word docs to WORD.zip
        Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, "WORD.zip")
        CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc", SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)

        ' 2) Rotating backup for DataDir
        Dim nextNum As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM"))
        Dim backupZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{nextNum}.zip")

        CreateZipForData(backupZip, AppPaths.DataDir)
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipFromPattern(zipPath As String, folder As String, pattern As String, opt As SearchOption)
        If Not Directory.Exists(folder) Then
            ' If the Word share doesn't exist, still create an empty zip so the process is predictable.
            CreateEmptyZip(zipPath)
            Return
        End If

        Dim files = Directory.GetFiles(folder, pattern, opt)

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    Dim entryName As String = Path.GetFileName(f)
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, entryName, CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipForData(zipPath As String, dataDir As String)
        If Not Directory.Exists(dataDir) Then
            CreateEmptyZip(zipPath)
            Return
        End If

        ' Match the spirit of the old backup: key business files in the data root.
        ' (No recursion by default.)
        Dim includePatterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT",
            "*.CUR",
            "PRC*.PRC",
            "PHONE*.LST",
            "BILLS.*",
            "PACK.NUM"
        }

        Dim files As New System.Collections.Generic.List(Of String)()

        For Each pat In includePatterns
            Try
                files.AddRange(Directory.GetFiles(dataDir, pat, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
            Catch
                ' ignore
            End Try
        Next

        ' De-dupe
        files = files.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList()

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    Dim entryName As String = Path.GetFileName(f)
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, entryName, CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateEmptyZip(zipPath As String)
        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                ' no entries
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    ' PACK.NUM behavior:
    ' - Stores a number (1..99)
    ' - Each run returns current value and increments it
    ' - Rolls over after 99 back to 1
    Private Function GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath As String) As Integer
        Dim n As Integer = 1

        Try
            If File.Exists(packNumPath) Then
                Dim txt As String = File.ReadAllText(packNumPath).Trim()
                If txt <> "" Then
                    Integer.TryParse(txt, n)
                End If
            End If
        Catch
            n = 1
        End Try

        If n < 1 OrElse n > 99 Then n = 1

        Dim nextN As Integer = n + 1
        If nextN > 99 Then nextN = 1

        Try
            File.WriteAllText(packNumPath, nextN.ToString())
        Catch
            ' If we can't write, still return n so backup can proceed.
        End Try

        Return n
    End Function

End Module
```

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        ' Ensure folders exist and migrate legacy root files (Skip rule)
        MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()

        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show($"Backup complete.{Environment.NewLine}{Environment.NewLine}Backups folder:{Environment.NewLine}{AppPaths.BackupDir}",
                                    "Backup",
                                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                                    MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message, "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "Y"
                ' Placeholder until we translate/replace the original CHAIN "EDBACKUP"
                NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup (needs spec/source)")

            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
```

Imports System.IO

Public Module AppPaths

    ' Base folder for this application's files on the Invoice server
    Public ReadOnly Property AppRoot As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu"

    ' All legacy/root files should live here going forward
    Public ReadOnly Property DataDir As String = Path.Combine(AppRoot, "Data")

    ' Zip outputs go here

messages.content:
No—**once a feature is fully ported to VB.NET**, it will **not** require the original DOS `.BAS` files at runtime.

What it *will* still require are the **data files** (whatever your business data is), but those are not “DOS BAS files”—they’re things like `*.DAT`, `*.CUR`, `*.LST`, etc., and we’re explicitly moving/standardizing those into:

- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\`

### What depends on the DOS `.BAS` files today
Right now, your WinForms project is **not loading or interpreting `.BAS`** at all. The only time DOS `.BAS` would still matter is if:
- you still run parts of the old system externally (e.g., via `SHELL`, `CHAIN` in BASIC, or launching a DOS EXE), or
- you haven’t ported a module yet and you’re using the old DOS program as a stopgap.

In the code we’ve written so far:
- The menu is pure WinForms.
- The backup (I) is pure .NET (`System.IO` + `System.IO.Compression`).
- The migration is pure .NET.

So **for (I)** specifically: **no `.BAS` needed**—only the files being backed up (data + Word docs).

### What about the “files currently existing in the root of Invoice”?
Those are in two categories:

1) **Legacy data files in `\\invoice\` root**  
   During the transition, we *may* still see them there. Our migration step moves them to `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\` (with your rule = **Skip if already exists**). After migration and once you confirm everything is working, you can keep the root clean.

2) **Legacy DOS program files (`.BAS`, old EXEs, batch files, etc.)**  
   The new app won’t need them **unless** you choose to keep launching them for unported options. Our current code is *not* launching them.

### Practical recommendation
- Keep the old DOS folder intact **until every menu option is ported and validated**.
- After that, the WinForms app should only depend on:
  - `\\invoice\MainMenu\...` (your app data + backups)
  - `\\invoice\word\...` (Word docs)

If you tell me which remaining menu options you plan to actually keep (vs retire), I can also tell you when it’s safe to delete the old DOS program files.
we can review that at the end of the project

Sounds good—we’ll revisit cleanup/decommissioning at the end.

For now, the working rule will be:

- **Ported features must not depend on any DOS `.BAS` files** (or running DOS programs).
- We’ll keep the legacy DOS files and any root files in place during the transition for safety, but the .NET app will progressively stop needing them as each module is replaced.

When you’re ready for the next step, tell me whether you want to:
1) add a small **progress UI** for the (I) backup (so it doesn’t look frozen), or
2) start porting the next feature (common next choices: **Rolodex (N)**, **Invoice Generator (B)**, or **Typewriter Mode (X)**).

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        ' Ensure folders exist and migrate legacy root files (Skip rule)
        MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()

        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show($"Backup complete.{Environment.NewLine}{Environment.NewLine}Backups folder:{Environment.NewLine}{AppPaths.BackupDir}",
                                    "Backup",
                                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                                    MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message, "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "Y"
                ' Placeholder until we translate/replace the original CHAIN "EDBACKUP"
                NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup (needs spec/source)")

            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")
            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time (placeholder)")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode (placeholder)")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case Else
                ' ignore
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

- `MigrationService`
- `BackupService`
- `AppPaths`

If those modules/files aren’t actually present in the project (or named differently), VB will error.

### Fast fix (so you can compile right now)
Use your working menu code, but change only **(I)** to call the backup later *without requiring new modules yet*. In other words: keep the UI stable, and we’ll add backup/migration after you confirm those files are added.

The only “regeneration” that fixes this **without requiring you to hunt duplicates** is to **remove the call entirely** and let the designer layout stand (i.e., don’t force the runtime layout from code). The menu will still build and buttons will still work.

- keeps your working menu/button code
- removes the problematic layout call and method (so no overload ambiguity)
- still supports KeyPress hotkeys
- still has the removed buttons (5, +, S, U, V, W) excluded

## 2) Add `MigrationService.vb` (MigrationRule = Skip)
```vbnet name=MigrationService.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO

Public Module MigrationService

    Private ReadOnly LegacyRoot As String = "\\invoice"

    Public Sub EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.AppRoot)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        Dim patterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "*.LST", "*.PRC",
            "PACK.NUM", "BILLS.*", "*.CHK", "*.TXT"
        }

        For Each pattern In patterns
            Dim files As String()

            Try
                files = Directory.GetFiles(LegacyRoot, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
            Catch
                Continue For
            End Try

            For Each src In files
                Dim dest As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, Path.GetFileName(src))

                ' Skip if already migrated
                If File.Exists(dest) Then Continue For

                Try
                    File.Move(src, dest)
                Catch
                    ' ignore (in use / permissions / etc.)
                End Try
            Next
        Next
    End Sub

End Module
```

## 3) Add `BackupService.vb` (uses ZIP built into .NET)
```vbnet name=BackupService.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.IO.Compression
Imports System.Linq

Public Module BackupService

    Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' 1) Word docs => WORD.zip
        Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, "WORD.zip")
        CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

        ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip
        Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
        Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
        Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

        CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipFromPattern(zipPath As String, folder As String, pattern As String)
        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                If Directory.Exists(folder) Then
                    For Each f In Directory.GetFiles(folder, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
                        archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                    Next
                End If
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateDataZip(zipPath As String, dataDir As String)
        Dim includePatterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "PRC*.PRC", "PHONE*.LST", "BILLS.*", "PACK.NUM"
        }

        Dim files As New System.Collections.Generic.List(Of String)

        For Each pat In includePatterns
            If Directory.Exists(dataDir) Then
                Try
                    files.AddRange(Directory.GetFiles(dataDir, pat, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
                Catch
                End Try
            End If
        Next

        files = files.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList()

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath As String) As Integer
        Dim n As Integer = 1

        Try
            If File.Exists(packNumPath) Then
                Integer.TryParse(File.ReadAllText(packNumPath).Trim(), n)
            End If
        Catch
            n = 1
        End Try

        If n < 1 OrElse n > 99 Then n = 1

        Dim nextN As Integer = n + 1
        If nextN > 99 Then nextN = 1

        Try
            File.WriteAllText(packNumPath, nextN.ToString())
        Catch
        End Try

        Return n
    End Function

End Module
```

Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        ' IMPORTANT:
        ' Your project currently has duplicate layout helpers in partial classes.
        ' To avoid "Overload resolution failed..." we do NOT call any EnsureLayout method here.
        ' Layout should be handled by the Designer (FormMainMenu.Designer.vb).
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: 5, +, S, U, V, W)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                        "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                        "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: 5, +, S, U, V, W

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case Else
                ' ignore
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

Your current `FormMainMenu.vb` looks good, and the way you hooked **I** is exactly what we want: UI stays simple, and the backup logic is isolated in `MigrationService/BackupService/AppPaths`.

Next things to do (in priority order):

## 1) Confirm the 3 support files exist and compile
Make sure your project has **exactly one** of each:
- `AppPaths.vb`
- `MigrationService.vb`
- `BackupService.vb`

If “I” runs but throws an exception, the most common issues will be permissions/sharing on `\\invoice\MainMenu\...` or missing access to `\\invoice\word`.

## 2) Update any leftover hardcoded paths (like `BackupConfig.vb`)
You showed `BackupConfig.vb` still has old defaults like `F:\WORD`, `C:\BACKUP`, `C:\PACK.NUM`.

If that class is still used anywhere, it will fight with the new UNC-based structure. You have two choices:
- **(A) Stop using `BackupConfig`** entirely (preferred if the new `BackupService` replaces it).
- **(B) Update `BackupConfig` defaults** to match the new locations:
  - WordSourceDir = `\\invoice\word`

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.IO.Compression
Imports System.Linq

Public Module BackupService

    Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' 1) Word docs => WORD.zip
        Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, "WORD.zip")
        CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

        ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip
        Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
        Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
        Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

        CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipFromPattern(zipPath As String, folder As String, pattern As String)
        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                If Directory.Exists(folder) Then
                    For Each f In Directory.GetFiles(folder, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
                        archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                    Next
                End If
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateDataZip(zipPath As String, dataDir As String)
        Dim includePatterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "PRC*.PRC", "PHONE*.LST", "BILLS.*", "PACK.NUM"
        }

        Dim files As New System.Collections.Generic.List(Of String)

        For Each pat In includePatterns
            If Directory.Exists(dataDir) Then
                Try
                    files.AddRange(Directory.GetFiles(dataDir, pat, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
                Catch
                End Try
            End If
        Next

        files = files.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList()

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath As String) As Integer
        Dim n As Integer = 1

        Try
            If File.Exists(packNumPath) Then
                Integer.TryParse(File.ReadAllText(packNumPath).Trim(), n)
            End If
        Catch
            n = 1
        End Try

        If n < 1 OrElse n > 99 Then n = 1

        Dim nextN As Integer = n + 1
        If nextN > 99 Then nextN = 1

        Try
            File.WriteAllText(packNumPath, nextN.ToString())
        Catch
        End Try

        Return n
    End Function

End Module
```

## 2) Make migration run only once (marker file)

In `MigrationService.vb`, add a marker file in `DataDir`, and skip migration if it exists.

### Drop-in `MigrationService.vb` (recommended)
Replace your whole `MigrationService.vb` with this:

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO

Public Module MigrationService

    Private ReadOnly LegacyRoot As String = "\\invoice"
    Private ReadOnly MarkerPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, ".migrated")

    Public Sub EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.AppRoot)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' If we've already migrated on this share, do nothing.
        If File.Exists(MarkerPath) Then Return

        Dim patterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "*.LST", "*.PRC",
            "PACK.NUM", "BILLS.*", "*.CHK", "*.TXT"
        }

        Dim movedAny As Boolean = False

        For Each pattern In patterns
            Dim files As String()

            Try
                files = Directory.GetFiles(LegacyRoot, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
            Catch
                Continue For
            End Try

            For Each src In files
                Dim dest As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, Path.GetFileName(src))

                ' Skip if already migrated
                If File.Exists(dest) Then Continue For

                Try
                    File.Move(src, dest)
                    movedAny = True
                Catch
                    ' ignore (in use / permissions / etc.)
                End Try
            Next
        Next

        ' Create marker even if nothing moved, so we don't keep scanning \\invoice\ forever.
        Try
            File.WriteAllText(MarkerPath, $"migrated={DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}")
        Catch
            ' If we can't write marker, migration will try again next time.
        End Try
    End Sub

End Module
```

Not really. The backup and one-time migration will work fine even if the UI message only says “Backup complete” and shows the folder.

It only matters if you want any of these:

- **Proof / audit:** users can tell exactly which ZIP was created (useful when there are many timestamped `WORD_*.zip` files).
- **Troubleshooting:** if Word ZIP succeeds but data ZIP fails, returning filenames makes it clearer what completed.
- **“Open the zip” convenience:** you can automatically open Explorer to the exact file.

If you don’t need any of that, keep it simple: leave `RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()` as `Sub` and just show the backup folder like you’re doing now.
just leave it

Then republish self-contained and redeploy to the workstations.
this is done

Good. Next, verify it behaves correctly on a workstation with a quick 60‑second checklist:

2) Press **I** twice:
   - In `\\invoice\MainMenu\Backups\` you should now see **two different** Word zips, like:

     - `WORD_2026-04-13_142645.zip`
   - And the rotating data zip should advance:

3) Confirm `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PACK.NUM` increments each run.

If any of those don’t happen, tell me exactly which item failed and what the error message said (full text), and we’ll tighten it up.

Great—at this point you’re in “deployment hardening” mode.

If you want the next safest improvements (optional, but helpful for multi‑PC):

1) **Prevent two PCs from writing the same BACKUP#.zip at the same time**  
   Add a simple lock file in `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\backup.lock` so concurrent runs don’t collide.

2) **Include `.docx` as well as `.doc`** (if your Word folder has modern files).

3) **Retain only the last N Word backups** (so the folder doesn’t grow forever).

Tell me which (if any) you want next and I’ll give you minimal code edits.
Prevent two PCs from writing the same BACKUP#.zip at the same time

Add a **network lock file** around the PACK.NUM read/advance + ZIP creation, so only one PC can run the backup at a time.

### Change: `BackupService.vb`
Add this helper method anywhere inside `BackupService` (near the bottom is fine):

```vbnet
Private Function AcquireBackupLock(lockPath As String) As FileStream
    ' Creating with FileShare.None makes it a cross-machine mutex on a UNC share.
    ' If another PC holds it, this throws an IOException.
    Return New FileStream(lockPath, FileMode.OpenOrCreate, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
End Function
```

Then update `RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()` to wrap the rotating BACKUP##.zip part with the lock:

```vbnet
Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
    Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
    Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

    ' 1) Word docs (timestamped) - can run without lock
    Dim stamp As String = DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss")
    Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"WORD_{stamp}.zip")
    CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

    ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip (LOCKED)
    Dim lockPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "backup.lock")

    Using lockStream As FileStream = AcquireBackupLock(lockPath)

        Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
        Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
        Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

        CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)

    End Using
End Sub
```

### What users will see if two PCs press **I** at the same time
The **second** one will likely fail with an error like “The process cannot access the file … backup.lock … because it is being used by another process.”

If you want a nicer message, we can catch `IOException` in `FormMainMenu` around the backup call and show “Backup already running on another PC—try again in a minute.”

If you paste your current `Case "I"` block, I’ll show the exact minimal `Catch` to add for that.
Every time I run this I get a windows smart screen dialogue, how can we stop that

You can’t reliably “code your way out” of SmartScreen. SmartScreen shows that dialog when the EXE is **unknown/untrusted** (no reputation) and/or is **marked as coming from the Internet**. To stop it, you typically need one (or both) of these fixes:

## 1) Remove the “Mark of the Web” (MOTW) from the files you copied
If you copy the published folder via browser download, email, some zip tools, etc., Windows tags the files and SmartScreen warns.

### Fix (manual, per workstation, easiest test)
1) On the workstation, right‑click **AMiOffice.exe** → **Properties**
2) If you see **Unblock** at the bottom, check it → **OK**

If you copied a whole folder of DLLs, they can be blocked too. Fastest approach:
- Put the published output into a **.zip** on your dev PC
- On the workstation: right‑click the zip → **Properties → Unblock** → OK
- Then extract the zip (everything inside will be unblocked)

## 2) Code-sign the EXE (best long-term fix)
For multiple PCs, the real “no prompts” solution is to **digitally sign** your EXE with a trusted certificate:
- Ideally an **EV code-signing certificate** (builds SmartScreen reputation much faster)
- Standard code-sign cert also helps, but SmartScreen reputation can still take time

Once signed, SmartScreen warnings usually go away (assuming the cert is trusted).

## 3) Deploy via an internal trusted method (domain/IT approach)
If these PCs are managed (domain/Intune), IT can reduce prompts by:
- AppLocker / Windows Defender Application Control allow rules
- Group Policy to trust your publisher certificate
- Distributing via Software Center/Intune, etc.

## Quick questions (so I can tell you the best path)
1) Are these workstations on a **Windows domain** (managed by an IT admin), or just standalone PCs?
2) How are you copying the app over right now: **USB**, **network share**, or **downloaded zip**?
stand alone and copying via network share

On standalone PCs copying via a network share, SmartScreen usually appears for one of two reasons: **the files have a “Mark of the Web”** (downloaded-at-some-point) or Windows simply has **no reputation** for the unsigned EXE. Here’s what you can do.

## 1) First check: is the EXE “blocked” (MOTW)?
On a workstation:
1) Right‑click `AMiOffice.exe` → **Properties**
2) If you see an **Unblock** checkbox/button at the bottom, click it → **OK**
3) Run again.

If you see “Unblock”, that’s the cleanest fix.

### Best practice when distributing updates
If you ever move the app around as a `.zip`:
- **Unblock the zip** *before* extracting:
  - Right‑click the `.zip` → Properties → **Unblock**
  - Then extract
This prevents every extracted file from being blocked.

## 2) If there is NO “Unblock”, SmartScreen is about reputation
If the EXE is **unsigned**, SmartScreen can still warn even when copied from a LAN share. The only durable way to stop that on unmanaged standalone PCs is:

### Code-sign the EXE
- Buy a code signing certificate (EV is best for SmartScreen reputation)
- Sign your published EXE as part of the build/publish process

Without signing, you may be stuck with “Run anyway” prompts on some machines.

## 3) Also avoid running directly from the network share
Even if you copy “via” the share, make sure users run it from a local folder like:
- `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\AMiOffice.exe`
Running *from* the share can increase security prompts and file-lock issues.

## Tell me what you see and I’ll give the exact next step
On one workstation, in `AMiOffice.exe` → **Properties**:
- Do you see an **Unblock** option (yes/no)?
- On the **Digital Signatures** tab, is it empty? (it will be empty if not signed)

With those two answers, I’ll tell you the quickest path to zero prompts for your exact case.
no unblock and no digital signatures tab

That means the EXE isn’t “MOTW-blocked”, and it’s also **unsigned**. In that situation, SmartScreen is warning because the app has **no publisher trust/reputation**. On standalone PCs, the practical ways to stop the dialog are:

## Best fix (recommended): code-sign the EXE
1) Buy a **code signing certificate** (EV is most effective at eliminating SmartScreen warnings quickly).
2) Sign the published `AMiOffice.exe` (and optionally your `.dll`s).
3) Redistribute the signed build — SmartScreen prompts should stop once Windows trusts the signature (EV usually helps immediately; standard cert can still require some reputation buildup).

If you want, tell me whether you can purchase a cert, and I’ll give you the exact `signtool.exe` command and where to hook it into your publish.

## If you can’t buy a cert: local allow-list per workstation
This doesn’t require a cert, but it’s manual on every PC:

### A) Windows Security “Reputation-based protection”
Some PCs allow turning off the specific SmartScreen check:
- Windows Security → App & browser control → Reputation-based protection settings
- turn off “Check apps and files” (or similar)

Downside: reduces security and must be done on every workstation (and users may not have admin rights).

### B) “Run anyway” once per new build
Even with no cert, SmartScreen often stops warning for the *same exact* binary after a user approves it—but it can reappear when you rebuild/publish (hash changes).

## Also: keep the EXE stable between builds
SmartScreen reputation is per binary hash/publisher. If you rebuild constantly, it never builds reputation. So:
- Publish less frequently, or
- Sign it (best).

## Two questions so I can recommend the path
1) Do you have an admin password for these standalone PCs (can you change Windows Security settings)?
2) Are you willing/able to purchase a code-signing certificate (roughly yearly cost), or do you need a free/manual workaround?
I have admin access. How much is a code-signing certificate per year

Typical cost is **hundreds of dollars per year**:

- **Standard code-signing certificate:** commonly about **$200–$500/year**.
- **EV (Extended Validation) code-signing certificate:** commonly about **$500–$1,000+/year**.

The exact price depends on vendor (DigiCert, Sectigo, GlobalSign, SSL.com, etc.), term length, and whether you buy multi‑year (often discounted per year).

Practical guidance for your situation (standalone PCs, small internal deployment):

- If you want SmartScreen warnings to go away **reliably and professionally**, especially on machines you don’t control forever, **EV code signing** is the most effective.
- If this app is only for your own PCs and you control them as admin, you can avoid the cost by using local policy/settings changes—but that reduces security and is per‑PC.

If you want, I can outline the “admin-only, no-certificate” approach that minimizes impact (e.g., allowing just this app path) and compare it to code signing.
that's way too expensive. let's implement the changes in windows security settings

You can do it, but the only built-in way that consistently stops the SmartScreen “Windows protected your PC” prompt for **unsigned** apps is to **turn off SmartScreen app checking** (system-wide) on each workstation. There isn’t a “trust just this one EXE” switch in Windows Security for SmartScreen.

## Change Windows Security settings (per workstation)

1) Open **Windows Security**
   - Start → type **Windows Security** → open it
2) Go to **App & browser control**
3) Click **Reputation-based protection settings**
4) Turn off (names vary slightly by Windows version):
   - **Check apps and files** → **Off**  *(this is the SmartScreen prompt you’re seeing)*

Optional (only if you’re still getting prompts in other contexts):
- **SmartScreen for Microsoft Edge** (not relevant if you’re just running an EXE)
- **Potentially unwanted app blocking** (doesn’t usually cause “protected your PC”)

After changing it, run `AMiOffice.exe` again.

## Important notes
- This reduces protection against unknown/untrusted apps **for everything on that PC**, not just AMiOffice.
- You’ll likely need to do this on **each workstation** (unless you later manage them centrally).

## Alternative (safer) admin workaround if you don’t want to disable SmartScreen
If the prompt is only happening because you run from a shared folder, a safer approach is:
- copy the app to `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\` and run locally (you already do this)
- keep the app in a fixed folder and restrict who can write to it

But if you’re seeing the *SmartScreen protected your PC* prompt even running locally, disabling “Check apps and files” is the lever.

## Tell me your Windows version
Which are these machines running: **Windows 10** or **Windows 11**?  
(The menu labels are a bit different; I can give the exact clicks for your version.)
ok, that fixed it, what next?

Next, lock in a **repeatable release + rollout process** so updates are consistent across all workstations.

## 1) Make a standard “Release package” folder each time
On your dev PC:
- Publish **Release / self-contained / win-x64** to a versioned folder, e.g.
  - `C:\Publish\AMiOffice\2026-04-13_01\`
- Zip that folder to a single file:
  - `AMiOffice_2026-04-13_01.zip`

This gives you a single artifact you can copy/store.

## 2) Rollout steps on each workstation (consistent)
1) Close AMiOffice (make sure it’s not running in Task Manager).
2) Rename the old folder (easy rollback):
   - `C:\Apps\AMiOffice` → `C:\Apps\AMiOffice_old`
3) Create fresh:
   - `C:\Apps\AMiOffice`
4) Copy in the new published files (or unzip).
5) Run `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\AMiOffice.exe`
6) Quick test:
   - press **I**
   - confirm new `WORD_*.zip` + next `BACKUP#.zip`

Rollback = rename folders back.

## 3) Optional but highly recommended next steps
Pick any you want:

A) **Add an “About / Version” screen** (shows build date/time + machine name).  
This prevents “am I on the latest build?” confusion.

B) **Centralize updates**: a simple `Update.bat` on each workstation that copies from `\\invoice\MainMenu\Deploy\Latest\` to `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\`.

C) **Logging**: write a small log file in `\\invoice\MainMenu\Logs\AMiOffice.log` when backups run (timestamp, machine, success/fail).

Which one do you want next: **A (version display)**, **B (update script)**, or **C (logging)**?
A) yes add an about / version screen

Add an **About** dialog that shows: app name, version, build date (from the assembly), and machine/user. Then hook it to a key (commonly **A** or **F1**), or add a visible “About” menu item if you have one.

Add a WinForms form named `FormAbout` with:

how do I create a new winform

In Visual Studio you add a WinForms form like any other item.

## Create a new WinForm (VB)
1) In **Solution Explorer**, right‑click your project **AMiOffice**.
2) Click **Add** → **New Item…**
3) In the list, choose **Windows Form** (sometimes shown as **Windows Form (Visual Basic)**).
4) Name it: `FormAbout.vb`
5) Click **Add**

Visual Studio will create the form designer file and open the designer.

## Add the controls
1) Open the designer for `FormAbout`.
2) From **Toolbox**, drag:
   - a **Label** onto the form
   - a **Button** onto the form
3) Set properties (click the control, press **F4**):
   - Label:
     - `Name` = `lblInfo`
     - `Dock` = `Fill` (or size it manually)
     - `AutoSize` = `False`
   - Button:
     - `Text` = `OK`
     - `DialogResult` = `OK`
     - (optional) `Anchor` = Bottom, Right
4) For the form itself:
   - `Text` = `About AMiOffice`
   - `StartPosition` = `CenterParent`
   - `AcceptButton` = your OK button

## Wire the OK button (if needed)
If you set `DialogResult = OK`, you usually don’t need any code—clicking it will close the dialog automatically.

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb

Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    ' TODO: customize these
    Private Const AuthorName As String = "YOUR NAME"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "you@example.com"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        ' "Build number" options:
        ' - File version (good if you set it in Assembly Information)
        ' - Informational version (good if you set it manually)
        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        ' Build date: last write time of the EXE on disk (simple + accurate for deployed build)
        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build Date (file time): {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author: {AuthorName}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        lblInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
    End Sub

End Class
```

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =

                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
