        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            AppTitle,
            "",
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",

            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",

            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",

            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        lblInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
    End Sub

End Class

The main title is covering the rest of the info

That’s a **WinForms docking order** issue: a `Dock=Fill` control (your RichTextBox) is being laid out before the `Dock=Top` title label, so it occupies the whole client area and the label ends up overlapping it.

Fix it by ensuring the controls are added in this order (top/bottom first, fill last), or by reordering in the designer.

## Fix in the Designer (fastest)
1) Open `FormAbout` in **Designer**.
2) Go to **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**.
3) In the outline, reorder the controls so they are like:

- `lblTitle` (Dock = Top)
- `pnlButtons` (Dock = Bottom)  *(if you added it)*
- `rtbInfo` (Dock = Fill)

In WinForms, the **Fill control must be last** (lowest in the outline / added last), otherwise it can cover others.

### How to reorder
In Document Outline, you can drag items up/down. If it won’t drag, use:
- Right‑click a control → **Bring to Front / Send to Back** until:
  - `rtbInfo` is **Sent to Back** (or `lblTitle` is brought to front)

## Quick property check
- `lblTitle.Dock = Top`
- `pnlButtons.Dock = Bottom`
- `rtbInfo.Dock = Fill`

After reordering, run again—the title should sit above the RichTextBox without covering it.

Add vertical space by inserting an empty line **at the top of the RichTextBox content** (and/or padding the RichTextBox). The overlap won’t be fixed by text, but this *will* push the “Author:” line down visually.

### Option B: add top padding inside the RichTextBox
This adds space above the first line without changing the text:

```vbnet
rtbInfo.Select(0, 0)
rtbInfo.SelectionIndent = 0
rtbInfo.SelectionRightIndent = 0
rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
rtbInfo.SelectionFont = rtbInfo.Font

' Add space by increasing the paragraph spacing
rtbInfo.SelectionCharOffset = 0
' (RichTextBox doesn't have true padding; the reliable way is blank lines or adjusting margins)
```

WinForms `RichTextBox` doesn’t have a real `Padding` property, so **blank lines are the most reliable**.

## Option 1 (recommended): ship the image as “Content” and load from disk
### 1) Add the image to the project

Else

```vbnet
Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)

    End If
End Using
```

## 1) Add the image and mark it Embedded Resource

2) Click `developer.png` in Solution Explorer → **Properties**:

## 3) Load the embedded image (robust way: auto-find the resource name)
Put this into `FormAbout.vb` (inside the class). It finds the resource by filename so you don’t have to guess the namespace:

```vbnet
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
' (you already import these)

Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
    Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

    ' Find any embedded resource that ends with "\developer.png" equivalent (resource names use dots)
    Dim resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".developer.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
               OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".developer.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

    If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
        picDeveloper.Visible = False
        Return
    End If

    Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
        If s Is Nothing Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        ' Copy to memory so the stream can be disposed safely
        Using ms As New MemoryStream()
            s.CopyTo(ms)
            ms.Position = 0
            picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
        End Using
    End Using
End Sub
```

Then call it at the top of `FormAbout_Load`:

```vbnet
LoadDeveloperImage()
```

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        ' Find any embedded resource that ends with "\developer.png" equivalent (resource names use dots)
        Dim resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _

                Return

The image isn’t loading for two reasons:

1) **`LoadDeveloperImage()` is never called** in `FormAbout_Load`.
2) Embedded resource names **don’t contain spaces** the way you’re checking. In .NET, the resource name will look like `YourDefaultNamespace.ESC_Logo.jpg` or `YourDefaultNamespace.Images.ESC_Logo.jpg` (spaces typically become `_`, and folders become extra `.parts`).

Here’s the smallest set of changes to make it work reliably.

## 1) Call `LoadDeveloperImage()` from `FormAbout_Load`
Put this near the top of `FormAbout_Load`:

```vbnet
LoadDeveloperImage()
```

## 2) Fix `LoadDeveloperImage()` to search by filename without assuming spaces
Replace your current `LoadDeveloperImage()` with this version:

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
    Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

    ' Match by filename, ignoring namespace/folders. Resource names use dots, and spaces are usually '_' in VB projects.
    Dim resName =
        names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
        OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESCLogo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
        OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
        OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.ToLowerInvariant().Contains("esc") AndAlso n.ToLowerInvariant().EndsWith(".jpg"))

    If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
        picDeveloper.Visible = False
        Return
    End If

    Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
        If s Is Nothing Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using ms As New MemoryStream()
            s.CopyTo(ms)
            ms.Position = 0
            picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
        End Using
    End Using
End Sub
```

## 3) One-time check: verify the actual embedded resource name
If it still doesn’t show, temporarily add this at the end of `FormAbout_Load` (just for troubleshooting):

```vbnet
rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine & "Embedded resources:" & Environment.NewLine &
                   String.Join(Environment.NewLine, Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetManifestResourceNames()))
```

Run the app, open About, and you’ll see the exact resource name. Paste that line here and I’ll give you the clean “hardcoded” `resName` that will always work.

In Solution Explorer click `ESC Logo.jpg` → Properties:
- **Build Action** must be **Embedded Resource**.

it won't open the about box now

That usually happens because the new resource-debug or image-load code is throwing an exception during `FormAbout_Load` (so the form fails while loading). The quickest fix is to make `LoadDeveloperImage()` **exception-safe** and (for now) avoid writing to `rtbInfo` before it’s ready.

Make these changes:

1) **Wrap `LoadDeveloperImage()` in `Try/Catch`** so it can’t crash the form.
2) Ensure `LoadDeveloperImage()` is called **after** `InitializeComponent()` (it is) and that `picDeveloper` exists on the form.

Replace your `LoadDeveloperImage()` with this safe version:

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Try
        If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        ' Look for your logo by filename (spaces usually become underscores)
        Dim resName =
            names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.ToLowerInvariant().Contains("esc") AndAlso n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                s.CopyTo(ms)
                ms.Position = 0
                picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
            End Using
        End Using

        picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        picDeveloper.Visible = True

    Catch ex As Exception
        ' Don't block the About box from opening
        If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        ' Optional: uncomment to see the real error while debugging
        ' MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
    End Try
End Sub
```

Also, make sure you have this import at the top (or fully-qualify `FirstOrDefault` logic another way):

```vbnet
Imports System.Linq
```

### While you’re at it (common cause)
Also confirm these two things (they’re the #1 causes):
1) In the designer, is the PictureBox **Name** exactly `picDeveloper`?
2) In Solution Explorer, click `ESC Logo.jpg` → Properties → is **Build Action = Embedded Resource**?

System.InvalidCastException: Conversion from string "" to type 'Boolean' is not valid.
 ---> System.FormatException: The input string '' was not in a correct format.
   at Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.Conversions.ParseDouble(String Value, NumberFormatInfo NumberFormat)
   at Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.Conversions.ToBoolean(String Value)
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.Conversions.ToBoolean(String Value)
   at AMiOffice.FormAbout.LoadDeveloperImage() in C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormAbout.vb:line 102

The crash is coming from **VB implicitly converting a String to Boolean** inside `LoadDeveloperImage()` (because `Option Strict Off` allows it). This almost always happens when code like this is used:

```vbnet
Dim resName = a OrElse b
```

where `a`/`b` are **strings**. `OrElse` is a Boolean operator, so VB tries to convert the string to Boolean → `""` can’t convert → `InvalidCastException`.

### Fix: don’t use `OrElse` to combine strings
Use an `If` chain (or `If(resName Is Nothing, ...)`) to pick the first match.

Also, **call `LoadDeveloperImage()`** from `FormAbout_Load` if you haven’t already.

Here’s a corrected `LoadDeveloperImage()` (no `OrElse` on strings):

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Try
        If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        Dim resName As String = Nothing

        resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                     n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                s.CopyTo(ms)
                ms.Position = 0
                picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
            End Using
        End Using

        picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        picDeveloper.Visible = True

    Catch
        If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
    End Try
End Sub
```

And make sure you have:

```vbnet
Imports System.Linq
```

### One more key point: your file has a space
Embedded resource names usually turn spaces into `_`, so the likely resource is `...ESC_Logo.jpg`. Your image file is **`ESC Logo.jpg`**, so that’s why we check for `.ESC_Logo.jpg`.

If this still doesn’t show the image, add this temporarily (once) to see the exact resource name:

```vbnet

```

Then we can hardcode the exact `resName`.

If you want, paste lines ~90–110 of your file (around line 102) and I’ll point to the exact expression that triggered the bad string→boolean conversion.
I want the picture to be between Build: and Author/Developer:

To place the picture **between** the “Build:” line and the “Author/Developer:” line, you should **stop using a single RichTextBox for everything** and instead split the About box into two sections:

- **Top RichTextBox** (or Label) with: Product / Version / File Version / Build
- **Middle panel** with: PictureBox + Author/Developer info
- **Bottom RichTextBox** with: machine info + shared paths (or keep it all in one bottom RTB)

A `RichTextBox` can’t reliably “insert an image between two lines of plain text” unless you start doing RTF/clipboard hacks; layout controls is the correct WinForms way.

### Recommended layout (simple)
1) Keep `lblTitle` Dock=Top
2) Add `rtbTop` Dock=Top (height ~90)
3) Add `pnlDev` Dock=Top (height ~110) containing:
   - `picDeveloper` Dock=Left (width ~96)
   - `lblDevInfo` Dock=Fill (TextAlign=MiddleLeft)
4) Keep your existing `rtbInfo` Dock=Fill for the rest (machine/shared paths)

#### Designer steps
- Add a new **RichTextBox**: `rtbTop`  
  - `Dock = Top`, `ReadOnly=True`, `BorderStyle=None` (optional), `Height=90`
- Add a new **Panel**: `pnlDev`  
  - `Dock = Top`, `Height=110`
- Inside `pnlDev` add:
  - `PictureBox` `picDeveloper` (`Dock=Left`, `Width=96`, `SizeMode=Zoom`)
  - `Label` `lblDevInfo` (`Dock=Fill`, `TextAlign=MiddleLeft`)
- Ensure dock order in Document Outline is:
  1. `lblTitle` (Top)
  2. `rtbTop` (Top)
  3. `pnlDev` (Top)
  4. `pnlButtons` (Bottom, if you have it)
  5. `rtbInfo` (Fill)

### Code changes (what to put where)
In `FormAbout_Load` build the “top” lines separately:

```vbnet
Dim topLines As New List(Of String) From {
    product,
    $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
    $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
    $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}"
}

rtbTop.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, topLines)
rtbTop.SelectAll()
rtbTop.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
rtbTop.DeselectAll()
```

Then load the image + developer text in the middle:

```vbnet
LoadDeveloperImage()

lblDevInfo.Text =
    $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Contact: {ContactEmail}"

' optional: center the label text too
lblDevInfo.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
```

And keep the rest in your existing `rtbInfo` (remove the author/company/phone/contact/year lines from it since they’re now in the middle section).

                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                     n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

Your code is *mostly* correct, and `LoadDeveloperImage()` is now written in a safe way (no more string `OrElse` problem). There are a couple fixes/cleanups I recommend so it behaves exactly as you intend.

## What’s wrong / should be changed

### 1) You’re writing “Embedded resources:” to `rtbInfo`, then immediately overwriting it
This block:

```vbnet
Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()
rtbInfo.Text = "Embedded resources:" & Environment.NewLine &
       String.Join(Environment.NewLine, names)
```

is **debug-only**, and it’s wasted work now because later you do:

```vbnet
rtbInfo.Clear()
rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
```

So either remove the debug block, or guard it with `#If DEBUG Then ... #End If`.

### 2) `buildDate` is calculated but never displayed
Not a bug, just unused. Either remove it or include it in `lines` (e.g., `"Build date: ..."`).

### 3) `LoadDeveloperImage()` only looks for `.jpg`
If you ever switch to PNG, it won’t load. Optional improvement: check `.png` too.

### 4) Swallowing exceptions silently can hide real issues
Your `Catch` is fine for production (don’t crash About box), but while you’re still verifying it, you may want to show the exception in DEBUG.

This removes the debug overwrite, optionally shows build date, and improves resource matching a bit.

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "About Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

I want to look at Placing a Title at the top of the main screen that looks just like the DOS version did

To make the top of your WinForms main screen look like a DOS title bar, the usual approach is: **add a docked header panel** and put a **single label** in it using a **monospace font** and DOS-like colors.

## What to build (WinForms)
1) On your main form (the “main screen” form), add a **Panel** named `pnlDosTitle`
- `Dock = Top`
- `Height = 28` (adjust to taste)
- `BackColor = Color.Navy` (classic DOS blue)

2) Add a **Label** inside it named `lblDosTitle`
- `Dock = Fill`
- `ForeColor = Color.White` (or Yellow)
- `BackColor = Color.Transparent` (or same as panel)
- `TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft`
- `Font = Consolas, 12, Bold` (or `Lucida Console`)

3) Set the text to match your DOS title style, for example:
- `" Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu "` (note the spaces)

## Code (put in your main form’s Load event)
```vbnet
Private Sub MainForm_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    lblDosTitle.Text = " Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu "
End Sub
```

## To match DOS even closer
- Use `Consolas` or `Lucida Console` (monospace)
- Use a solid background (Navy/Blue)
- Add a thin bottom border line:
  - easiest: set `pnlDosTitle.BorderStyle = BorderStyle.FixedSingle`
  - or draw a 1px line in the panel’s Paint event

Add a Panel named pnlHeader:    I'm having trouble with this, does the panel get placed onto tlproot, or flpleft?

Put the header **outside** both `tlpRoot` and `flpLeft`.

In other words: add `pnlHeader` **directly onto the Form** (the main menu form’s surface), then set `pnlHeader.Dock = Top`, and set your existing `tlpRoot.Dock = Fill` (or keep it, but make sure it ends up filling the remaining space).

### Why
- `tlpRoot` and `flpLeft` are for your menu content/layout.
- The DOS-style title line is a **separate top band** that should span the whole width and stay fixed.

### Exact designer steps
1) Open the main menu form in **Design** view.
2) Click a blank area of the form (so nothing inside `tlpRoot`/`flpLeft` is selected).
3) Drag a **Panel** from the Toolbox onto the form.
4) Set these properties on the panel:
   - **Name**: `pnlHeader`
   - **Dock**: `Top`
   - **Height**: `48` (or similar)
   - **BackColor**: `Black`

5) Make sure your existing root layout control is docked to fill:
   - Click `tlpRoot`
   - Set **Dock** = `Fill`

### If the panel keeps ending up inside `tlpRoot`
That means `tlpRoot` is covering the entire form and intercepting the drop.

Two easy fixes:

**Fix A (Document Outline – easiest)**
1) Open **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**
2) Drag `pnlHeader` so it becomes a sibling of `tlpRoot` under the form:
   - `FormMainMenu`
     - `pnlHeader`
     - `tlpRoot`

**Fix B (cut/paste)**
1) If the panel accidentally landed inside `tlpRoot`, select it.
2) **Ctrl+X** (cut)
3) Click the form background (or select the form in Document Outline)
4) **Ctrl+V** (paste)
5) Set `Dock = Top`

### Important: control order matters with Docking
For Dock to behave correctly, `pnlHeader` should be “above” the fill control. If you see weird overlap:
- Right-click the form → **Order → Bring to Front** on `pnlHeader`
- Or in Document Outline, ensure `pnlHeader` is listed before `tlpRoot`.

This almost always means `pnlHeader` is either (a) **inside the wrong container** (e.g., inside `flpLeft`), (b) **behind** `tlpRoot` due to docking/order, or (c) its **Height is effectively 0** / it’s not docked.

Do these checks in this order (they’re quick and will pinpoint it):

## 1) Verify the control hierarchy (most important)
Open **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**.

You should see something like:

- `FormMainMenu` (or whatever your main form is)
  - `pnlHeader`
    - `lblMainMenu`
    - `lblDateTime`
  - `tlpRoot` (or your main layout)

If `pnlHeader` is under `tlpRoot` or `flpLeft`, that’s the issue.

### Fix
In Document Outline, **drag `pnlHeader`** so it is a **direct child of the form**, *above* `tlpRoot`.

## 2) Verify Dock settings
Click each control and confirm properties:

### `pnlHeader`
- `Dock = Top`
- `Height = 48` (or at least ~30)
- `BackColor = Black` (so you can see it)

### `tlpRoot`
- `Dock = Fill` (not Top)
- It should **not** be Dock=Fill *and* “covering” the header due to order (next step handles that)

## 3) Fix docking order (header can be hidden even if Dock is right)
Even with correct Dock, **z-order matters**.

- Select `pnlHeader` in the designer
- **Right-click → Bring to Front**

Also try:
- Select `tlpRoot`
- **Right-click → Send to Back**

## 4) Make sure labels aren’t invisible
Click `lblMainMenu`:
- `ForeColor = Gold/Yellow`
- `BackColor = Black` or `Transparent`
- `Dock = Left` (or None is OK, but Dock is easiest)
- `Width` not tiny (e.g., 250)

Click `lblDateTime`:
- `ForeColor = Gold/Yellow`
- `Dock = Fill` (or Right)
- `TextAlign = TopRight` or `MiddleRight`

## 5) Confirm the code is running on the correct form
Make sure the `Load` handler is on the **startup main menu form**, not another form.

In your main menu form code, add this temporarily:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    MessageBox.Show("Main menu form loaded")
    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub
```

If you **don’t** see that message box, you edited the wrong form (or the form isn’t the startup form).

## 6) Quick “force visible” test
Temporarily set the header to something unmistakable:

- `pnlHeader.BackColor = Color.Red`
- `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.White`

If you still can’t see it, it’s definitely hierarchy/order/docking.

In WinForms, when you have Dock=Top and Dock=Fill, the order the controls are added matters. The easiest way to correct it:

Look at (and edit) **`FormMainMenu.vb`** — the one that opens when you click **View Code** on the form in Solution Explorer. That is the *code-behind* where event handlers like `FormMainMenu_Load`, `tmrClock_Tick`, and `UpdateHeaderClock()` belong.

Here’s what each of the things you listed means, and which one matters.

## The files you should care about

### 1) `FormMainMenu.vb`  ✅ (this is where you put the code)
- Contains `Public Class FormMainMenu`
- This is where you add:
  - `Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load ... Handles MyBase.Load`
  - `Private Sub tmrClock_Tick ... Handles tmrClock.Tick`
  - `Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()`

You should **not** have multiple *different* `FormMainMenu.vb` files in the same project unless they’re different names.

### 2) `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`  ⚠️ (don’t edit by hand)
- Auto-generated by the WinForms designer
- Contains the control declarations and `InitializeComponent()`
- This is where the panel/labels/timer are *declared* and created
- You normally **never edit** this manually (use the Designer instead)

### 3) `FormMainMenu.vb (Design)*` ✅ (this is the Designer view)
- This is not a separate file; it’s the *designer surface* for `FormMainMenu.vb`
- You use this to drag/drop `pnlHeader`, labels, timer, etc.

### 4) `FormMainMenu.vb*` ✅ (same file, unsaved changes)
- The `*` just means “unsaved changes”
- It’s still the same `FormMainMenu.vb` file.

## The odd one: `FormMainMenu (working version after removing ...).vb`
This is a **separate VB file** (a copy/backup) and **may not be the form that is actually running**.

Two possibilities:
- It’s just a spare backup file (not used by the project), OR
- It’s included in the project and might even define another form/class, which can cause confusion.

FormMainMenu.vb: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - True GUI menu (no terminal emulation).
    ' - Assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - This code will enforce the layout at runtime.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (unchanged)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: S, U, V, W, +, 5

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")
            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick

    End Sub

End Class

Got it—this `FormMainMenu.vb` explains exactly why you don’t see the header: **your `EnsureLayout()` method is removing every control on the form except `tlpRoot`.** So even if `pnlHeader` is correctly added in the Designer, it gets removed at runtime on load.

Specifically, this block deletes the header:

```vbnet
For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
    Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
    If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
        Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
    End If
Next
```

### What to change (minimum fix)
Modify `EnsureLayout()` so it keeps both `pnlHeader` and `tlpRoot`, and make sure the docking order is correct (header Top, root Fill).

Here’s the corrected `EnsureLayout()` section to replace the “remove everything” portion with:

```vbnet
' Ensure tlpRoot is on the form
If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
    Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
End If

' Keep the header panel if present
If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing AndAlso Not Me.Controls.Contains(pnlHeader) Then
    Me.Controls.Add(pnlHeader)
End If

' Docking
If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing Then
    pnlHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
    pnlHeader.BringToFront()

Imports System

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - True GUI menu (no terminal emulation).
    ' - Assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - This code will enforce the layout at runtime.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        ' Ensure tlpRoot is on the form
        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        ' Keep the header panel if present
        If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing AndAlso Not Me.Controls.Contains(pnlHeader) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(pnlHeader)
        End If

        ' Docking
        If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing Then
            pnlHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            pnlHeader.BringToFront()
        End If

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.SendToBack()
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (unchanged)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: S, U, V, W, +, 5

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")
            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick

    End Sub

End Class

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class

Imports System.IO

Public Module MigrationService

    Private ReadOnly LegacyRoot As String = "\\invoice"
    Private ReadOnly MarkerPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, ".migrated")

    Public Sub EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.AppRoot)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' If we've already migrated on this share, do nothing.
        If File.Exists(MarkerPath) Then Return

        Dim patterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "*.LST", "*.PRC",
            "PACK.NUM", "BILLS.*", "*.CHK", "*.TXT"
        }

        Dim movedAny As Boolean = False

        For Each pattern In patterns
            Dim files As String()

            Try
                files = Directory.GetFiles(LegacyRoot, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
            Catch
                Continue For
            End Try

            For Each src In files
                Dim dest As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, Path.GetFileName(src))

                ' Skip if already migrated
                If File.Exists(dest) Then Continue For

                Try
                    File.Move(src, dest)
                    movedAny = True
                Catch
                    ' ignore (in use / permissions / etc.)
                End Try
            Next
        Next

        ' Create marker even if nothing moved, so we don't keep scanning \\invoice\ forever.
        Try
            File.WriteAllText(MarkerPath, $"migrated={DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}")
        Catch
            ' If we can't write marker, migration will try again next time.
        End Try
    End Sub

End Module

Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.IO.Compression
Imports System.Linq

Public Module BackupService

    Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' 1) Word docs => WORD_yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss.zip (timestamped)
        Dim stamp As String = DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss")
        Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"WORD_{stamp}.zip")
        CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

        ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip (LOCKED)
        Dim lockPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "backup.lock")

        Using lockStream As FileStream = AcquireBackupLock(lockPath)

            Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
            Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
            Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

            CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)

        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipFromPattern(zipPath As String, folder As String, pattern As String)
        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                If Directory.Exists(folder) Then
                    For Each f In Directory.GetFiles(folder, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
                        archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                    Next
                End If
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateDataZip(zipPath As String, dataDir As String)
        Dim includePatterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "PRC*.PRC", "PHONE*.LST", "BILLS.*", "PACK.NUM"
        }

        Dim files As New System.Collections.Generic.List(Of String)

        For Each pat In includePatterns
            If Directory.Exists(dataDir) Then
                Try
                    files.AddRange(Directory.GetFiles(dataDir, pat, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
                Catch
                End Try
            End If
        Next

        files = files.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList()

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath As String) As Integer
        Dim n As Integer = 1

        Try
            If File.Exists(packNumPath) Then
                Integer.TryParse(File.ReadAllText(packNumPath).Trim(), n)
            End If
        Catch
            n = 1
        End Try

        If n < 1 OrElse n > 99 Then n = 1

        Dim nextN As Integer = n + 1
        If nextN > 99 Then nextN = 1

        Try
            File.WriteAllText(packNumPath, nextN.ToString())
        Catch
        End Try

        Return n
    End Function
    Private Function AcquireBackupLock(lockPath As String) As FileStream
        ' Creating with FileShare.None makes it a cross-machine mutex on a UNC share.
        ' If another PC holds it, this throws an IOException.
        Return New FileStream(lockPath, FileMode.OpenOrCreate, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
    End Function

End Module

## 3) Fix `FormMainMenu.vb` (the code-behind)

### Important: choose ONE layout owner
Right now you have mixed responsibilities:
- Designer creates tlpRoot with rtbScreen (1 column)
- Code intends 2 columns with flpLeft/flpRight

You should pick one:

#### Recommended: keep layout in designer, simplify EnsureLayout
If you fix the designer so tlpRoot already has flpLeft/flpRight set correctly, then `EnsureLayout()` should only do:

Your “working version” contains the clock code, but it’s incorrectly named:

That name is fine, but it’s inside a partial class that already overrides `OnLoad`, and it duplicates load logic across partial files.

    Private Sub BuildMenu()

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

In your screenshot, the buttons are being created correctly, but they’re **far too narrow**, so the text is getting clipped and you only see the first part (e.g., `(A)`). On the right column you can see a little extra like `(Y) Ed`, which confirms it’s purely a **width/layout** problem, not a font/color problem. (Also note your “Main / Menu” header label is wrapping because it’s narrow too.) ![image2](image2)

### Why it’s happening
Your `ResizeButtonsToPanel()` uses `panel.ClientSize.Width`, but you call it **before the layout has finalized**, so the `ClientSize.Width` is still small. Then the buttons keep that tiny width.

---

## Fix (do this): resize after the form finishes laying out
Replace your `OnLoad` with this version (or add the shown lines). The key parts are:
- call `BeginInvoke` so resizing runs **after** WinForms layout completes
- also handle panel `SizeChanged` so it keeps correct widths

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnLoad(e)

    Me.KeyPreview = True

    BuildMenu()

    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()

    ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
    Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                  ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                  ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                              End Sub))
End Sub
```

### Add these two event handlers (recommended)
They ensure if the user resizes the window (or scrollbars appear), button widths stay correct:

```vbnet
Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
    ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
End Sub

Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
    ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
End Sub
```

## Also fix one thing in `ResizeButtonsToPanel`
You’re subtracting only 5 pixels; that’s a bit tight when scrollbars appear. Update this line:

```vbnet
Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6
```

Full method:

```vbnet
Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
    If panel Is Nothing Then Return

    Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

    If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

    For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
        Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
        Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

        If btn IsNot Nothing Then
            btn.Width = targetWidth
        ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
            lbl.Width = targetWidth
        End If
    Next
End Sub
```

Example `AddMenuButton` with the key parts:

```vbnet
Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
    Dim btn As New Button()

    btn.AutoSize = False
    btn.Height = 44   ' optional: increase height so bigger font fits
    btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))

    btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
    btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
    btn.Tag = key
    btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

    btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
    btn.BackColor = Color.Black
    btn.ForeColor = Color.White
    btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
    btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
    btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
    btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
    btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

    btn.AutoEllipsis = True

    AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

    panel.Controls.Add(btn)
End Sub
```

Can the time at upper right be changed to 12 hr format with am or pm

Yes—change the time format string in your header update code.

In **`FormMainMenu.vb`**, find where you set the header date/time text (likely in `UpdateHeaderClock()`), and change the time portion from 24-hour (`HH:mm:ss`) to 12-hour with AM/PM (`hh:mm:ss tt`).

Example:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

    ' 12-hour time with AM/PM:
    lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:hh:mm:ss tt}"
End Sub
```

This will display like: `02:16:06 PM` instead of `14:16:06`.

If you want “AM/PM” lowercase (`am/pm`), WinForms formats `tt` based on culture; we can force lowercase with `.ToLowerInvariant()` on just the time part if needed.

```vbnet
{now:hh:mm:ss tt}
```
